title: ForensicReport schema upgrade + forensic.ts buildAssertions/buildCandidateFiles
description: Upgrade ForensicReport schema by replacing recommendations_for_skill: string[] with ForensicAssertion[] (structured assertions with type/statement/confidence/evidence/coverage/proposed_rule) and adding candidate_files: CandidateFileEntry[] plus sampling_budget. Rewrite forensic.ts to produce buildAssertions() + buildCandidateFiles() replacing buildSkillRecommendations(). This is the foundational data contract that makes Check-not-Ask (Matcha) interaction possible — without structured confidence tiers and evidence anchors, the SKILL cannot make implicit-accept decisions for HIGH assertions.

files.change: Add ForensicAssertion interface/schema (type, statement, confidence: HIGH|MEDIUM|LOW, evidence[], coverage: {ratio, total, matched, co_occurring_patterns[]}, proposed_rule?, alternatives?). Add CandidateFileEntry interface/schema (path, family: entry|component|config|test|domain, rationale). Add sampling_budget type ({max_files: 15, max_lines_per_file: 100}). Extend ForensicReport to include assertions: ForensicAssertion[], candidate_files: CandidateFileEntry[], sampling_budget. Keep recommendations_for_skill as DEPRECATED optional field for one version migration window.

Add buildAssertions(frameworkKind, topology, codeSamples) producing ForensicAssertion[] with quantified coverage ratios: HIGH = ratio≥0.8 + co_occurring≥2 OR AST-level marker (@ccclass); MEDIUM = 0.5-0.8 OR single pattern; LOW = <0.5 OR conflict. Add buildCandidateFiles(topology, codeSamples, entryPoints) producing CandidateFileEntry[] grouped by pattern family (entry/component/config/test/domain), top-3 per family, total ≤ 12. Upgrade inferPatternHint() to return structured {pattern, confidence, evidence_lines, co_occurring} instead of string. Mark buildSkillRecommendations() as DEPRECATED, keep it for one version, add comment pointing to buildAssertions().

implementation: Read packages/shared/src/schemas/forensic-report.ts to understand current schema before modifying
Define ForensicAssertion, CandidateFileEntry, CandidateFileFamily types and Zod schemas in forensic-report.ts; run tsc to verify type correctness
Extend ForensicReport interface and forensicReportSchema to include assertions[], candidate_files[], sampling_budget; keep recommendations_for_skill as optional deprecated
Read packages/cli/src/scanner/forensic.ts lines 215-323 to understand existing buildCodeSamples/buildSkillRecommendations/inferPatternHint before rewriting
Implement buildAssertions() with quantified confidence formula (ratio threshold + co_occurring count OR AST-level marker); each assertion must carry evidence[] with file:line anchors
Implement buildCandidateFiles() with per-family top-3 selection logic across entry/component/config/test/domain families; enforce ≤ 12 total cap
Upgrade inferPatternHint() to return structured object; update callers
Mark buildSkillRecommendations() deprecated with JSDoc; update main buildForensicReport() to populate assertions[] and candidate_files[] alongside deprecated field
Run tsc --noEmit from packages/cli to verify no type errors

reference.pattern: Existing Zod schema definitions in packages/shared/src/schemas/
reference.examples: Follow Zod object/enum/array pattern from existing forensicReportSchema; follow coverage ratio logic from conclusions.json key_conclusions[2]

criteria: ForensicAssertion fields type/statement/confidence/evidence[]/coverage/proposed_rule?/alternatives? all present in schema and interface
CandidateFileEntry fields path/family/rationale all present; family enum = entry|component|config|test|domain
forensicReportSchema Zod validates a JSON containing assertions[], candidate_files[], sampling_budget without errors
buildAssertions() on werewolf-minigame-stub produces ≥ 5 assertions; each assertion carries type/statement/confidence/evidence with ≥ 1 file:line anchor
HIGH assertion on werewolf: coverage.ratio ≥ 0.8 AND co_occurring_patterns.length ≥ 2 (cocos-creator framework detection)
buildCandidateFiles() on werewolf-minigame-stub produces ≥ 3 families of candidates; total candidate_files.length ≤ 12
sampling_budget = {max_files: 15, max_lines_per_file: 100} present in ForensicReport output
recommendations_for_skill still emitted (deprecated) so old SKILL.md consumers do not break
tsc --noEmit passes with zero type errors in packages/shared and packages/cli

rationale.chosen_approach: Add new typed fields (assertions[], candidate_files[], sampling_budget) alongside deprecated recommendations_for_skill; implement buildAssertions/buildCandidateFiles in forensic.ts consuming upgraded inferPatternHint
alternatives_considered: Replace recommendations_for_skill in-place (breaks existing SKILL.md consumers before migration window)
Keep string[] and add a separate enrichment step (two-pass approach adds complexity)
decision_factors: SKILL.md is rewritten in TASK-002 to consume ForensicAssertion[]
One-version migration window prevents silent breaks
CLI-side confidence inference is cheap and testable unlike SKILL-side inference
rationale.tradeoffs: Keeping deprecated field for one version means forensic.ts temporarily emits both; minor maintenance overhead during transition

manual_checks: Run fab init on examples/werewolf-minigame-stub and inspect .fabric/forensic.json for assertions[] and candidate_files[] fields
Verify at least one assertion has confidence=HIGH with coverage.ratio≥0.8
success_metrics: tsc --noEmit exits 0 for packages/shared and packages/cli
forensicReportSchema.parse() succeeds on werewolf fixture forensic.json output
buildAssertions() emits ≥ 3 HIGH confidence assertions for cocos-creator project
unit: forensic-report.test.ts: schema parse test for ForensicAssertion + CandidateFileEntry
forensic.test.ts: buildAssertions() confidence formula unit tests (high/medium/low thresholds)
forensic.test.ts: buildCandidateFiles() family distribution and cap enforcement
integration: forensic.test.ts: buildForensicReport() on werewolf-minigame-stub produces assertions[] and candidate_files[] in output

risks.description: Coverage ratio calculation requires counting files of the same type, which may be imprecise for heterogeneous projects
risks.mitigation: Use extension-based file classification (same .ts extension in same directory) as first approximation; document known limitation in JSDoc
risks.fallback: Fall back to co_occurring pattern count only (≥3 patterns = HIGH) if ratio calculation is unreliable

risks.description: buildCandidateFiles top-3 per family selection may produce overlapping files across families
risks.mitigation: De-duplicate by path after family selection; if a file matches multiple families, assign to the highest-priority family

interfaces.name: ForensicAssertion
interfaces.definition: { type: 'framework'|'pattern'|'invariant'|'domain'; statement: string; confidence: 'HIGH'|'MEDIUM'|'LOW'; evidence: Array<{file: string; line: string; snippet: string}>; coverage: {ratio: number; total: number; matched: number; co_occurring_patterns: string[]}; proposed_rule?: string; alternatives?: string[] }
interfaces.purpose: Structured assertion consumed by SKILL Phase 1 for batch Architecture Review display

interfaces.name: CandidateFileEntry
interfaces.purpose: Pre-packaged file list for SKILL Phase 0 active recon within 15×100 token budget

key_functions.signature: buildAssertions(frameworkKind: string, topology: TopologyResult, codeSamples: CodeSampleResult[]): ForensicAssertion[]
key_functions.purpose: Produces structured assertions with quantified confidence from scanner results

key_functions.signature: buildCandidateFiles(topology: TopologyResult, codeSamples: CodeSampleResult[], entryPoints: ForensicEntryPoint[]): CandidateFileEntry[]
key_functions.purpose: Selects top-3 representative files per pattern family (≤12 total) for SKILL supplemental read
